dimensions.rationale: The candidate-bound receipt is LOCAL_SYNTHETIC_ONLY and records zero remote writes and no protected execution.
The claimed closed v1.0.4-to-v1.0.5 transition is not mechanically exact: schema validation accepts both mismatched version/finding combinations, and the reviewed builder cannot emit v1.0.5.

Closure would require post-review edits to the status model/builder, yet those truth-authority surfaces are absent from post-review drift protection; a final status can therefore be changed without review-bound integrity.
The reviewed work is local, records no external mutation, and removed ephemeral outputs, so Git remains the rollback boundary.

findings.finding: Candidate, request, reply, model-family, task-ID, receipt, and four-attempt identities are mechanically represented and checked.
findings.resolution_ref: The v2 provenance schema and validator require four ordered attempts per family, exact identities, eight distinct task IDs, shared final candidate/request/receipt bindings, and distinct final replies.

findings.finding: The local gate receipt is bound to the exact candidate and preserves the complete 15-command result set with output digests and scope counters.
findings.resolution_ref: Receipt3 has the supplied exact hash and candidate/tree, and the validator rejects incomplete command IDs, failed commands, sanitized-output digest drift, path leakage, and sensitive patterns.

findings.finding: Prospective repository, LFS, object, and release-asset demand is projected and cumulative write demand is reserved before effectful callbacks.
findings.resolution_ref: CapacityDemand and CapacityPolicy project every bounded dimension; OperationalGate rejects absent or unsafe demand and reserves it before execution, including uncertain failures.

findings.finding: The previously omitted workflow and evidence-capture paths are now included in post-review drift detection.
findings.resolution_ref: All workflow paths and capture_candidate_gates.py are protected, with temporary-Git regression cases for production-workflow and capture-tool drift.

findings.finding: The two root-detected defects are repaired: historical RMD-03/RMD-04 assertions permit a later successor, and production composition hash-binds the added capacity/Kill/gate/runtime regression surfaces.
findings.resolution_ref: Historical tests retain immutable manifest checks while accepting later current status, and the exact production source map now covers the prospective-capacity, Kill, operational-gate, production, and decisive test files.

findings.finding: The v1.0.5 closure transition remains bypassable and is not candidate-complete. delivery-status-v1.schema.json independently enumerates package_version and resolved_review_findings, so all four cross-product combinations validate, including v1.0.4 with REV-P1-006 and v1.0.5 without it. The candidate builder and model are still hard-pinned to v1.0.4/pre-closure, while build_delivery_status.py and delivery_status_model.json are omitted from post-review protected prefixes, so the required final v1.0.5 truth-authority change would be unreviewed and could encode a mismatched closure state without this assurance validator blocking it.
findings.required_fix: Encode package_version and resolved_review_findings as two coupled schema alternatives; add negative tests for both mismatched cross-product states; make the reviewed deterministic builder/model able to derive only the exact pre-closure or hash-bound final-closure state; and protect those status-authority surfaces from post-review drift before issuing a new candidate/request/receipt and fresh reviews.

limitations: This read-only static review did not execute protected Oracles, real Gmail/private-repository operations, production workflows, deployment, or publication.
Receipt3 proves only retained local synthetic evidence; repository hashes and task IDs require the retained Codex thread audit log and are not signed third-party attestations.
No production health, final AC-033, RMD-06, or RMD-07 readiness is inferred.
summary: FAIL: the prior evidence, capacity, workflow-drift, and root-detected composition/history defects are resolved, but the v1.0.5 status transition is schema-bypassable and requires unreviewed changes to unprotected truth-authority code, leaving RMD05-CLOSURE-003 open.
